question: What did Anthropic's CEO say in early 2025 about DeepSeek's claim that its model cost only about $6 million to train?
must_contain: Dario Amodei's late-January 2025 essay response: the ~$6M figure is misleading (it covers only the final training run), DeepSeek did not 'do for $6M what cost US companies billions', and he used the episode to argue for stronger chip export controls.
facts: Amodei's essay 'On DeepSeek and Export Controls' was published in late January 2025
Amodei disclosed Claude 3.5 Sonnet cost 'a few tens of millions' to train, 9-12 months before DeepSeek-V3
He characterized DeepSeek's model as close to US models 7-10 months older, at lower but not revolutionary cost
hardness_note: One search surfaces SemiAnalysis cost estimates and video headlines, not Amodei's actual argument or quotes.
answer:
Here is a detailed answer based on the sources I retrieved.

---

## What Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei Said About DeepSeek's ~$6M Training Cost

On **January 29, 2025**, Anthropic CEO **Dario Amodei** published a blog post titled **"On DeepSeek and Export Controls"** (also shared on X that day) that directly addressed the widely circulated claim that DeepSeek trained its V3 model for only about $6 million. His key points were as follows:

### 1. "DeepSeek does not 'do for $6M what cost US AI companies billions'"

Amodei explicitly pushed back on the framing that DeepSeek had achieved a paradigm-shattering cost breakthrough. He argued that the narrative was based on a misunderstanding of what the $6 million figure actually represented.

### 2. The $6M figure is for *pre-training compute only*, taken at face value

Amodei noted he was taking the $6 million from DeepSeek's paper **"at face value"** as the cost of the **pre-training run** for DeepSeek-V3. However, he stressed a critical distinction:

> **"The cost to train a specific model (which is the $6M)"** vs. **"the overall cost of R&D (which is much higher)."**

He pointed out that DeepSeek had reportedly accumulated ~**50,000 Hopper GPUs** (Nvidia H800 chips), representing a hardware investment on the order of **~$1 billion**. The $6 million covers only one run of pre-training compute, not the total research and development expenditure behind the model.

### 3. DeepSeek's cost is "on-trend" — not a breakthrough

Amodei contextualized the cost by comparing it to Anthropic's own models. **Claude 3.5 Sonnet**, he said, is a mid-sized model that cost **"a few $10M's to train"** and was trained **9–12 months prior** (so around early-to-mid 2024). DeepSeek-V3 was trained in **November/December 2024**.

Given the historical trend of training costs falling by roughly **~4x per year**, Amodei calculated: *"we'd expect a model 3-4x cheaper than 3.5 Sonnet/GPT-4o around now."* His conclusion was that DeepSeek-V3 represents **"an expected point on an ongoing cost reduction curve"** — not a unique technological leap.

### 4. Broader geopolitical context

Beyond the cost dispute, Amodei used the post to argue that DeepSeek's progress underscored why **export controls on advanced chips to China** are **"even more existentially important"** than they were a week earlier. He said he does not view DeepSeek's researchers as adversaries themselves — *"in interviews they've done, they seem like smart, curious researchers who just want to make useful technology"* — but warned that they operate under an authoritarian government that would be "far more unfettered" if it matched the US in AI capability.

### Summary

In short, Amodei's position was that the $6 million training-cost figure was real but **narrow** (covering only pre-training compute), that the cost was **expected** given the pace of efficiency improvements in the industry, and that it did **not** represent an existential challenge to the economics of US AI companies — but it did make export controls more urgent.

**Sources:**
- Dario Amodei's blog post: [On DeepSeek and Export Controls](https://darioamodei.com/on-deepseek-and-export-controls)
- CNBC coverage: [Anthropic CEO doesn't see DeepSeek as 'adversaries,' but says export controls are critical](https://www.cnbc.com/2025/01/29/anthropic-ceo-amodei-says-he-doesnt-view-deepseek-as-adversaries.html)
- VentureBeat analysis: [Dario Amodei challenges DeepSeek's $6 million AI narrative](https://ramaonhealthcare.com/dario-amodei-challenges-deepseeks-6-million-ai-narrative-what-anthropic-thinks-about-chinas-latest-ai-move)
- Amodei's X post linking the blog: [@DarioAmodei/status/1884636410839535967](https://x.com/DarioAmodei/status/1884636410839535967)

question: How did Linus Torvalds respond to Christoph Hellwig's claim that Rust code had been merged into the Linux kernel over his objections as DMA maintainer?
must_contain: Torvalds's February 2025 rebuttal: the disputed pull request did not touch the DMA layer at all — it merely used the DMA API from a separate subdirectory — and a maintainer does not get to control where his subsystem is used.
facts: The dispute was over Rust bindings calling the kernel's DMA API, February 2025
Torvalds wrote the pull request 'DID NOT TOUCH THE DMA LAYER AT ALL'
Torvalds rejected the position 'as a DMA maintainer I control what the DMA code is used for' as 'not how any of this works'
hardness_note: One search describes the drama's existence but not the content of Torvalds's rebuttal.
answer:
Linus Torvalds responded to Christoph Hellwig's objections in a detailed email on the Rust-for-Linux mailing list on **February 20–21, 2025**, after Hellwig publicly claimed that Torvalds had privately told him he "absolutely is going to merge Rust code over a maintainer's objection" [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/software/2025/02/21/linux-royalty-backs-adoption-of-rust-for-kernel-code/1359453). Below is the full substance of Torvalds' reply, drawn from the complete email text published by [Phoronix](https://www.phoronix.com/news/Torvalds-On-Rust-Maintainers).

---

### 1. The pull request never touched Hellwig's code

Torvalds began by refuting the factual basis of Hellwig's objection:

> *"The fact is, the pull request you objected to DID NOT TOUCH THE DMA LAYER AT ALL. It was literally just another user of it, in a completely separate subdirectory, that didn't change the code you maintain in _any_ way, shape, or form."*

He called Hellwig's arguments "complete garbage" and said the objection was not about code changes but about controlling who may *use* the DMA API.

---

### 2. Maintainers do not control who uses their code

Torvalds directly addressed the core principle Hellwig had invoked:

> *"Honestly, what you have been doing is basically saying 'as a DMA maintainer I control what the DMA code is used for'. And that is not how *any* of this works."*

He drove the point home with a hypothetical:

> *"What's next? Saying that particular drivers can't do DMA, because you don't like that device, and as a DMA maintainer you control who can use the DMA code? That's _literally_ exactly what you are trying to do with the Rust code."*

Then he gave an unequivocal ruling:

> *"So let me be very clear: if you as a maintainer feel that you control who or what can use your code, YOU ARE WRONG."*

---

### 3. "You can't have it both ways"

Torvalds rejected the idea that a maintainer could simultaneously ignore Rust and veto Rust code that uses their C interfaces:

> *"You can't say 'I want to have nothing to do with Rust', and then in the very next sentence say 'And that means that the Rust code that I will ignore cannot use the C interfaces I maintain'."*

He framed the trade-off explicitly:

> *"You are not forced to take any Rust code, or care about any Rust code in the DMA code. You can ignore it. But 'ignore the Rust side' automatically also means that you don't have any *say* on the Rust side."*

---

### 4. The "wall of protection" goes both ways

Torvalds acknowledged the existing promise that C developers who want nothing to do with Rust would be shielded from Rust-related work. But he argued that protection cuts both ways:

> *"If you don't want to deal with the Rust code, you get no *say* on the Rust code."*

> *"Put another way: the 'nobody is forced to deal with Rust' does not imply 'everybody is allowed to veto any Rust code'."*

---

### 5. The choice for maintainers: engage or stay out

Torvalds laid out two paths for subsystem maintainers:

- **Engage** with the Rust side — become, in effect, a co-maintainer of the Rust bindings, and thereby have a voice in how they look.
- **Stay out** — ignore the Rust code entirely, but forfeit any say over it.

He also noted that in practice it need not be "all or nothing": a maintainer could be "aware of the Rust bindings, and willing to work with the Rust side, but perhaps not hugely actively involved."

---

### 6. Professional respect despite the disagreement

Torvalds ended with a personal note:

> *"I respect you technically, and I like working with you. And no, I am not looking for yes-men, and I like it when you call me out on my bullshit. I say some stupid things at times, there needs to be people who just stand up to me and tell me I'm full of shit. But now I'm calling you out on *YOURS*."*

---

### Aftermath: Hellwig steps down as DMA maintainer

Following Torvalds' intervention, **Christoph Hellwig removed himself as a maintainer of the DMA mapping subsystem.** The change was merged into the Linux **6.14** kernel tree. The commit message read: *"Marek has graciously offered to maintain the dma-mapping tree."* **Marek Szyprowski** took over as sole maintainer. Hellwig remains a co-maintainer of the NVMe driver, NVMe target, personality handling, and FreeVXFS filesystem areas [Phoronix](https://www.phoronix.com/news/Hellwig-DMA-Helpers-Removed). The Rust DMA bindings themselves are expected to be merged for the **Linux 6.15** release [LWN](https://lwn.net/Articles/1011197).

[Note: stopped early — time limit reached]

question: How did Sam Altman publicly respond to Elon Musk's $97.4 billion takeover bid for OpenAI, and what counter-offer did he joke about?
must_contain: Altman rejected the February 2025 bid on X with a joke counter-offer to buy Twitter for $9.74 billion, and said Musk was probably just trying to slow OpenAI down.
facts: Altman posted 'no thank you but we will buy twitter for $9.74 billion if you want'
The Musk-led bid was $97.4 billion, February 2025
At the Paris AI summit Altman told Bloomberg Musk was 'probably just trying to slow us down' and said he took the offer 'not particularly' seriously
hardness_note: One search shows the rejection happened but the $9.74B Twitter joke never appears in snippets.
answer:
Sam Altman publicly responded to Elon Musk's $97.4 billion unsolicited bid for the nonprofit that controls OpenAI with a terse, sarcastic post on X (the platform formerly known as Twitter) on Monday, February 10, 2025. He wrote:

> **"no thank you but we will buy twitter for $9.74 billion if you want."**

[Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/elon-musk-reportedly-offers-974-billion-for-openai-sam-altman-says-no-thank-you-215634193.html) | [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/elon-musk-investors-offering-97point4-billion-control-openai-rcna191572) | [Variety](https://variety.com/2025/biz/news/open-ai-sam-altman-elon-musk-reject-ai-1236303087)

The counter-offer Altman joked about was **buying Twitter (now X) for $9.74 billion** — exactly one-tenth of Musk's $97.4 billion bid, and far below the $44 billion that Musk himself paid to acquire Twitter in 2022. The quip was a pointed jab at Musk's own overpriced purchase of the platform.

Musk replied minutes later, calling Altman a **"swindler"**, and in a separate reply called him **"Scam Altman"** [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/business-news/elon-musk-investors-offering-97point4-billion-control-openai-rcna191572). The exchange was the latest flashpoint in a long-running feud between the two men, who co-founded OpenAI as a nonprofit in 2015 but have since become bitter rivals, with Musk now suing OpenAI to block its conversion to a for-profit entity and operating his own competing AI startup, xAI.

question: Which all-time Oscar record did 'Sinners' break at the 2026 nominations, and what first did its cinematographer achieve at the ceremony?
must_contain: Sinners set a new all-time record with 16 nominations (previous record 14, shared by three films), and cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman ever to win Best Cinematography.
facts: Sinners earned a record 16 nominations at the 98th Academy Awards, surpassing 14
Autumn Durald Arkapaw was the first woman to win Best Cinematography
Sinners won 4 Oscars including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan's dual role
hardness_note: The nomination record surfaces in one search; the cinematography first does not.
answer:
Here are the answers to your questions, based on verified reporting from the 2026 (98th) Academy Awards.

---

## 1. The all-time Oscar record 'Sinners' broke

**'Sinners' earned 16 nominations at the 98th Academy Awards, the most nominations ever received by a single film in Oscar history.** It broke the previous record of 14 nominations, which had been held jointly by three films: *All About Eve* (1950), *Titanic* (1997), and *La La Land* (2016). The nominations were announced on January 22, 2026, and the film went on to win four Oscars at the ceremony on March 15, 2026.

*Sources: [Screendaily](https://www.screendaily.com/news/oscars-2026-sinners-leads-with-all-time-record-16-nominations/5212899.article), [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/01/22/sinners-oscar-nominations-record-coogler-jordan), [Hollywood Reporter](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/sinners-wins-four-oscars-record-16-nominations-1236533090)*

---

## 2. The first achieved by 'Sinners' cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw

**Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman ever to win the Oscar for Best Cinematography, and also the first Black person to win in that category, at the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026.** She was the first woman of color ever nominated in the category (only three women had been nominated before her: Rachel Morrison for *Mudbound*, Ari Wegner for *The Power of the Dog*, and Mandy Walker for *Elvis*). During her acceptance speech, she asked all the women in the audience to stand, saying, "I really want all the women in the room to stand up, because I feel like I don't get here without you guys."

*Sources: [PBS News](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/arts/autumn-durald-arkapaw-makes-history-as-first-woman-cinematographer-to-win-an-oscar), [ABC7 Los Angeles](https://abc7.com/post/sinners-cinematographer-autumn-durald-arkapaw-makes-oscars-history-1st-woman-win-category/18719260), [Instagram/IFP](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DV78ywzjSru?hl=en)*

---

**In summary:** *Sinners* set the all-time record for most Oscar nominations (16) for a single film, and its cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history at the ceremony as the first woman and first Black person to win the Best Cinematography Oscar.

question: How many combined points and goals were scored in Super Bowl LX and the 2026 UEFA Champions League final (including extra time)?
must_contain: 44 — 42 points in Super Bowl LX (29-13) plus 2 goals in the Champions League final (1-1 after extra time; the shootout does not count).
facts: Super Bowl LX ended 29-13 (42 points)
The 2026 UCL final ended 1-1 after extra time before penalties
Combined total: 44
hardness_note: Two unrelated events; no source combines them, and the shootout-vs-goals distinction requires care.
answer:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the answer.

**Super Bowl LX** (February 8, 2026, at Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara, CA):
- Seattle Seahawks 29, New England Patriots 13
- Total points scored: 29 + 13 = **42 points**
- No "goals" are scored in American football (only touchdowns, field goals, extra points, and safeties)

**2026 UEFA Champions League Final** (May 30, 2026, at Puskás Aréna, Budapest):
- Paris Saint-Germain 1–1 Arsenal after extra time (PSG won 4–3 on penalties)
- Goals scored in regular time + extra time: PSG's Ousmane Dembélé (1 goal) + Arsenal's Kai Havertz (1 goal) = **2 goals**
- Penalty shootout goals are not counted in the final scoreline

**Combined total:**
- Total points in Super Bowl LX: **42**
- Total goals in UCL final (including extra time): **2**
- Combined (points + goals): **44**

---

### Combined total

- **Super Bowl LX total points (Seattle Seahawks 29, New England Patriots 13):** 42 points
- **2026 UEFA Champions League final total goals (regular time + extra time; PSG 1–1 Arsenal):** 2 goals (Kai Havertz for Arsenal in the 5th minute; Ousmane Dembélé penalty for PSG in the 60th minute)
- **Grand total (points + goals):** 44

### Score details

| Event | Date | Venue | Final Score (incl. extra time) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Super Bowl LX** | Feb 8, 2026 | Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara, CA | Seattle Seahawks 29 – New England Patriots 13 |
| **2026 UEFA Champions League Final** | May 30, 2026 | Puskás Aréna, Budapest | Paris Saint-Germain 1–1 Arsenal (4–3 pens) |

### Sources

- Super Bowl LX final score 29–13: [IndyStar](https://www.indystar.com/story/sports/nfl/2026/02/09/who-won-super-bowl-2026-last-night-seahawks-patriots-what-super-bowl-lx-score-superbowl-60-mvp/88586307007/) and [Pats Pulpit](https://www.patspulpit.com/new-england-patriots-scores-results/121674/super-bowl-2026-live-updates-news-recap-discussion-seattle-seahawks)
- 2026 Champions League final 1–1 after extra time (PSG 4–3 on pens): [Olympics.com](https://www.olympics.com/en/news/football-uefa-mens-champions-league-final-2026-arsenal-psg-results)
- Matching Champions League final score confirmed: [ESPN](https://www.espn.com/soccer/schedule/_/league/uefa.champions)
- Wikipedia listing affirming 1–1 after extra time: [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_UEFA_Champions_League_final) (confirmed via snippet, 403 on full page fetch)

question: What was the result of the opening match of the 2026 World Cup, and what disciplinary record did that match set?
must_contain: Mexico beat South Africa 2-0 at Estadio Azteca on June 11, 2026, in a match with three red cards — the most ever in a World Cup opening match.
facts: Mexico 2-0 South Africa, June 11, 2026, Estadio Azteca; scorers Julián Quiñones (9') and Raúl Jiménez
Three red cards were shown (two South Africa, one Mexico) — a record for a World Cup opener
It was Mexico's first ever win in a World Cup opening match (previously 5 losses, 2 draws)
hardness_note: The score surfaces in one search; the red-card record and first-opener-win angle do not.
answer:
Here are the details of the 2026 World Cup opening match and the disciplinary record it set.

---

## Opening Match Result: Mexico 2–0 South Africa

The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicked off on **Thursday, June 11, 2026**, at the **Estadio Azteca** in Mexico City — the first stadium ever to host matches at three different men's World Cups (1970, 1986, and 2026). The co-hosts **Mexico defeated South Africa 2–0** in front of a packed crowd [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards) [Yahoo Sports](https://sports.yahoo.com/soccer/live/world-cup-day-1-scores-results-korea-tops-czechia-after-wild-finish-mexico-handles-south-africa-in-ugly-affair-150000044.html).

**Goal scorers:**
- **Julián Quiñones** (Mexico) — 9th minute, scoring the first goal of the entire tournament
- **Raúl Jiménez** (Mexico) — 67th minute, with a free header

**Referee:** Wilton Sampaio (Brazil)

---

## Disciplinary Record: Most Red Cards in a World Cup Opener

The match set a new record for the **most red cards issued in a World Cup opening match**, with **three players sent off** — more than any curtain-raiser in tournament history [FOX Sports](https://www.foxsports.com/stories/soccer/mexico-vs-south-africa-world-cup-opener-makes-history-three-red-cards) [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).

### The three red cards in detail

1. **Sphephelo Sithole (South Africa) — 49th minute** – Sent off for denial of an obvious goal-scoring opportunity (DOGSO) after tripping Mexico's Brian Gutiérrez as he ran through on goal. Sithole became the **first player since Bolivia's Marco Etcheverry (32 years earlier)** to be sent off in a World Cup opener [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).

2. **Themba Zwane (South Africa) — 84th minute** – Sent off for violent conduct after striking Mexico's Roberto Alvarado in the face during an off-the-ball scuffle. The referee initially missed the incident but was called to the pitchside monitor by VAR (Video Assistant Referee Nicolás Gallo of Colombia) and issued a red card. At age 36, Zwane became the **second-oldest player ever sent off in a World Cup**, behind only the USA's Fernando Clavijo (age 38 vs. Brazil in 1994) [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards) [Ratopati](https://english.ratopati.com/story/66387/world-cup-2026-begins-with-a-shower-of-red-cards-are-we-going-back-to-the-old-days).

3. **César Montes (Mexico) — 90+2' stoppage time** – Sent off for a professional foul on South Africa's Khuliso Mudau, who was charging down the right wing. The tackle was judged as denial of a goal-scoring opportunity, and the VAR upheld the on-field call without needing a monitor review [FOX Sports](https://www.foxsports.com/stories/soccer/mexico-vs-south-africa-world-cup-opener-makes-history-three-red-cards) [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).

### Broader context of the record

- **First World Cup opener with 3 red cards** — The previous record for an opening match was 2 red cards, set in 1990 when Cameroon beat Argentina 1–0 with nine men [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).
- **Only 6 red cards have ever been awarded in World Cup openers** across all 22 tournaments, and 5 of those came in just two matches (this one and 1990's Cameroon–Argentina) [FOX Sports](https://www.foxsports.com/stories/soccer/mexico-vs-south-africa-world-cup-opener-makes-history-three-red-cards).
- **Most red cards in a single World Cup match since 2006** — The last time a World Cup match saw 3+ red cards was the infamous "Battle of Nuremberg" (Portugal 1–0 Netherlands, 2006 Round of 16), which had 4 red cards and 16 yellow cards [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).
- **More red cards than the entire 1978 tournament** — This single match produced more red cards than the whole 1978 World Cup. It also equaled the pace of the 2018 and 2022 tournaments, which each had only 4 red cards across 64 matches [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards) [Ratopati](https://english.ratopati.com/story/66387/world-cup-2026-begins-with-a-shower-of-red-cards-are-we-going-back-to-the-old-days).

Remarkably, despite the three red cards, the match featured only **23 fouls** — described as "clumsy challenges rather than cynical ones" [The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7352942/2026/06/11/world-cup-mexico-south-africa-red-cards).

question: What was the tallest building completed in 2024? Explain why sources disagree.
must_contain: Both candidates and the discrepancy: CTBUH/Skyscraper Center credit the Iconic Tower in Cairo (393.8 m); early-2024 forecast lists named Chongqing's International Land-Sea Center (458.2 m), whose completion is counted differently or slipped.
facts: CTBUH's data lists Iconic Tower, Cairo (393.8 m, Africa's first supertall) as 2024's tallest completion
2024 forecast articles projected the International Land-Sea Center, Chongqing (458.2 m) as the year's tallest
The disagreement hinges on whether the Chongqing tower's completion is dated 2024
hardness_note: One search returns all-time-tallest listicles; neither candidate nor the discrepancy surfaces.
answer:
根据主要权威机构的说法，2024年竣工的最高建筑是埃及开罗的**标志塔**，高394米（1,292英尺）。然而，不同来源之间存在分歧，原因如下：

**权威答案：标志塔（开罗）——394米**

高层建筑与城市人居委员会（CTBUH）——全球公认的高层建筑排名机构——在其**2025年趋势与预测**报告中明确指出：“非洲进入超高层建筑领域，开罗的标志塔成为2024年最高的竣工建筑”[CTBUH 2025 Trends & Forecasts](https://elevatorworld.com/article/ctbuh-2025-trends-forecasts)。CTBUH的SkyscraperCenter.com数据库也将标志塔列为2024年竣工，高度为394米（1,292英尺），共77层，用途为酒店/住宅/办公[SkyscraperCenter.com](https://www.skyscrapercenter.com/buildings)。维基百科证实，该塔的混凝土结构工程于2021年7月完工，随后于2021年8月24日封顶，整个建筑于**2024年竣工**，正式超越阿尔及利亚的大清真寺宣礼塔，成为非洲最高建筑[Wikipedia - Iconic Tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iconic_Tower_(Egypt))。

**为什么存在分歧？**

**1. 未实现的预测：重庆国际陆海中心（458米）**

2024年初，多个来源（包括The Real Deal/SkyScraperCentral和The B1M）预测，重庆的**国际陆海中心**（458米，1,503英尺）将成为2024年竣工的最高建筑[The Real Deal](https://therealdeal.com/national/2024/02/04/these-are-the-10-tallest-skyscrapers-to-be-completed-in-2024)。然而，这座建筑**并未在2024年竣工**。尽管其主体结构已于2022年6月封顶，但根据新华社报道，其幕墙工程直至**2025年4月**才正式完工[english.news.cn](https://english.news.cn/20250410/b193f223470246b7bc2cb88bf3003c14/c.html)。因此，这些预测是基于当时的施工进度表，但实际竣工时间已推迟至2025年。

**2. CTBUH内部数据矛盾：山东国际金融中心（428米）**

CTBUH的**2024数据手册**将山东国际金融中心（济南，428米，88层）列为2024年竣工，使其成为比标志塔（394米）更高的建筑[CTBUH Data Handbook 2024 PDF](https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/cloud.ctbuh.org/shop/DataHandbook_2024.pdf)。然而，其他证据表明该建筑可能并未在2024年完全竣工：
- 维基百科指出，该建筑于**2025年1月**才达到其最终高度428米，成为山东省最高建筑[Wikipedia - Shandong IFC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shandong_IFC)。
- 另一个来源（Archinect）称其“预计于2026年竣工”[Archinect](https://archinect.com/anlab/project/greenland-shandong-international-financial-center)。
- CTBUH的**2025年趋势与预测**报告明确指出标志塔是“2024年最高的竣工建筑”，这暗示CTBUH本身可能已追溯性地修正了其数据，或采用了不同的“竣工”定义。

**3. “竣工”的不同定义**

高层建筑行业对“竣工”的定义存在分歧：
- **主体封顶**（建筑达到结构最高点）通常发生在实际竣工前数年。例如，国际陆海中心于2022年封顶，但直到2025年才完成幕墙工程。
- **建筑竣工**（外墙完工、电梯运行、可入住）是CTBUH用于排名的标准。标志塔在2024年达到了这一标准，而国际陆海中心和山东国际金融中心则未达到。
- **预测性排名**：一些媒体和网站（如SkyScraperCentral）会发布基于施工进度表的*预测性*排名，但这些预测常常未能实现。

**4. 地域偏见与报道范围**

中国媒体和建筑刊物通常更关注中国项目，而全球出版物则可能更关注标志塔的历史意义——它是非洲第一座超高层建筑（300米以上）。这种报道偏差可能导致某些排名中不同建筑的曝光度存在差异。

**总结：2024年竣工的最高建筑**

| 建筑 | 地点 | 高度 | 2024年实际竣工？ | 来源 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **标志塔** | 开罗，埃及 | **394米** | **是**（CTBUH确认） | CTBUH 2025趋势与预测；SkyscraperCenter.com；维基百科 |
| 山东国际金融中心 | 济南，中国 | 428米 | 可能为否（2025年1月封顶；2026年竣工） | 维基百科；Archinect |
| 国际陆海中心 | 重庆，中国 | 458米 | 否（2025年4月幕墙完工） | 新华社 |

**判定：** 根据高层建筑与城市人居委员会（CTBUH）——全球高层建筑排名最权威的机构——2024年竣工的最高建筑是埃及开罗的**标志塔**，高394米。关于国际陆海中心（458米）和山东国际金融中心（428米）的争议源于预测性排名、不同的“竣工”定义以及实际施工进度的延迟。

question: How many moons does Saturn have? Why do reputable sources give different numbers?
must_contain: There is no single stable number: 274 after the IAU recognized 128 new moons in March 2025, higher counts reported since (285 or more during 2026), older references say 83-146 — and the count depends on recognition date plus an unsettled definition of 'moon'. Any current count is acceptable if the answer explains the recognition-date dependence.
facts: The IAU recognized 128 new Saturnian moons on March 11, 2025, bringing the total to 274 at that time
The new moons were found by stacking Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope images; most are retrograde, 1-20 km
Counts published since are higher still (285 or more during 2026) because newly recognized moons keep being added — the number depends on the recognition date
hardness_note: Different counts surface in one search, but reconciling which is current and why (recognition dates, definition debate) requires reading.
answer:
## How many moons does Saturn have?

As of the most recent official count from the **International Astronomical Union (IAU)**, confirmed by its Minor Planet Center in **March 2026**, Saturn has **285 moons**[IAU Minor Planet Center Confirms New Moons](https://www.iau.org/IAU/IAU/News/Ann2026/MPC-New-Moons-Saturn-Jupiter.aspx). This was reached after 11 additional moons were added to the 274 that had been confirmed in March 2025[The Guardian - 128 new moons](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2025/mar/11/astronomers-discover-128-new-moons-orbiting-saturn)[NASA Space Place](https://spaceplace.nasa.gov/how-many-moons).

A brief timeline of the recent rapid increases:

- **Before 2019:** Saturn's count sat in the 60s–80s.
- **2019:** 20 new moons discovered, bringing the total to 82.
- **May 2023:** 62 new moons announced, taking the count to 146 (then surpassing Jupiter)[BBC Future](https://www.bbc.com/future/article/20230703-why-saturns-moons-have-remained-hidden-from-view).
- **March 11, 2025:** 128 new moons officially recognized by the IAU, bringing the total to **274**[Space.com](https://www.space.com/the-universe/saturn/saturn-officially-has-128-more-moons)[NASA](https://science.nasa.gov/saturn/moons/).
- **March 2026:** 11 additional moons confirmed, raising the total to **285**[IAU Minor Planet Center Confirms New Moons](https://www.iau.org/IAU/IAU/News/Ann2026/MPC-New-Moons-Saturn-Jupiter.aspx).

Saturn now has far more confirmed moons than all other planets in the Solar System combined. For comparison, Jupiter has 101 (as of March 2026), Uranus 28, Neptune 16, Mars 2, and Earth 1.

---

## Why do reputable sources give different numbers?

There are several concrete reasons why you will see different numbers (e.g., 62, 83, 146, 247, 274, 285) depending on the source and the date of publication:

### 1. **The count changes rapidly as new discoveries are officially confirmed**
This is the primary reason. The number of known Saturnian moons has exploded in just the last few years. A source published before May 2023 might list ~83 moons; one from late 2023 might show ~146; one from before March 2025 might show ~146; one from mid-2025 might show 274; and as of March 2026, the official count is 285. Many websites, including NASA's, are not updated instantly. For example, as of this writing, NASA's Saturn moons page still lists "274 confirmed moons" while the IAU has already moved to 285[NASA Saturn Moons](https://science.nasa.gov/saturn/moons/)[Facebook Star Walk](https://www.facebook.com/StarWalkApp/posts/1384842130349965).

### 2. **The difference between "confirmed" and "provisional" or "candidate" moons**
The IAU's Minor Planet Center is the official body that validates moon discoveries. New candidates must be tracked over multiple observations to confirm they are in stable, planetocentric orbits (not just passing asteroids). Some sources count only fully confirmed moons; others may include "candidate" or "provisional" objects that have not yet been formally recognized. This was especially true in earlier years when NASA's planetary fact sheets would list "53 confirmed moons with 9 awaiting confirmation"[Astronomy Stack Exchange](https://astronomy.stackexchange.com/questions/13419/why-does-the-planet-saturn-have-numerous-62-moons-compared-to-the-rest-of-the).

### 3. **What even counts as a "moon"? — There is no formal definition**
Surprisingly, there is **no official, universally accepted definition** of what constitutes a moon. As Dr. Edward Ashton, the lead discoverer of many of the new moons, has said: *"I don't think there's a proper definition for what is classed as a moon. There should be."*[The Guardian](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2025/mar/11/astronomers-discover-128-new-moons-orbiting-saturn). NASA's working description is: *"naturally formed bodies that orbit planets are called moons"*[The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/saturn-now-has-274-moons-but-exactly-what-makes-something-a-moon-remains-unclear-252033). But this raises ambiguous edge cases:
- Should every individual ice chunk in Saturn's rings (there are millions) be considered a moon?
- Should a 1–2 km irregular rock high above the ring plane count the same as Titan (5,150 km in diameter)?
- Do captured asteroids that are only a few kilometers across qualify?

Different sources may implicitly or explicitly draw the line differently, especially when distinguishing between "ring moonlets" and proper "moons."

### 4. **The distinction between "regular" and "irregular" moons**
Saturn has 24 **regular moons** (large, spherical bodies that formed with Saturn and orbit in the equatorial plane) and now 261 **irregular moons** (small, potato-shaped objects, just a few kilometers across, captured from elsewhere and often in highly tilted or retrograde orbits)[The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/saturn-now-has-274-moons-but-exactly-what-makes-something-a-moon-remains-unclear-252033). Some older sources or educational materials may only count the major regular moons, inadvertently undercounting.

### 5. **Observational limits — we are likely nearing the detection ceiling with current technology**
All of the newly discovered moons are tiny (1–5 km across) and extremely faint (about 100 million times fainter than the naked eye can see). They were found using the "shift and stack" technique on the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope. Ashton has stated: *"With current technology I don't think we can do much better than what has already been done."*[Space.com](https://www.space.com/the-universe/saturn/saturn-officially-has-128-more-moons). So the count will likely stabilize until a new generation of telescopes (e.g., the Vera Rubin Observatory) comes online.

### 6. **Websites have different update schedules**
Many popular science websites, encyclopedias, and even NASA pages are not updated in real time. A source showing "274" may simply not have been updated since the March 2026 IAU announcement adding 11 more. For example, the Facebook post from Star Walk notes: *"NASA’s Saturn page still reflects the previous official total of 274, so you may still see that number in some places."*[Facebook Star Walk](https://www.facebook.com/StarWalkApp/posts/1384842130349965).

---

**In summary:** As of today (July 9, 2026), the most authoritative, up-to-date number is **285 moons**, confirmed by the IAU Minor Planet Center in March 2026. Discrepancies you see are almost always due to differing publication dates, the lag in updating web pages, the difference between confirmed vs. candidate moons, and the fact that there is no formal, universally agreed definition of what a "moon" actually is.

[Note: stopped early — iterations limit reached]

question: What is the population of Nigeria, and why is the number contested?
must_contain: Official/UN-derived estimates put it around 230-240 million, but the figure is contested: Nigeria hasn't held a census since 2006, its own National Population Commission admits it lacks accurate figures, and credible critics argue the true number may be tens of millions lower.
facts: UN-based estimates are ~240 million (2026); the last census was 2006
The National Population Commission has publicly admitted it lacks accurate population figures
Skeptics estimate the real figure could be 170-180 million; a biometric census has been planned
hardness_note: One search returns a single estimate; the institutional story (census gap, NPC admission, overcounting incentives) requires more.
answer:
## Population of Nigeria

As of mid-2026, Nigeria's population is estimated at **approximately 241–242 million**. The United Nations (World Population Prospects 2024 revision) projects a figure of **242,431,832** for the year 2026, while Worldometer's live counter on 14 April 2026 shows **241,251,257** [Worldometer](https://www.worldometers.info/world-population/nigeria-population). The World Bank's estimate for 2024 was **232,679,478** [Trading Economics / World Bank](https://tradingeconomics.com/nigeria/population-total-wb-data.html). Multiple sources project that Nigeria crossed 237 million in 2025 [The Guardian Nigeria](https://guardian.ng/news/nigerias-population-to-cross-237-million-by-2025-report). Nigeria is the **6th most populous country in the world**, with a median age of about **18.3 years** and a population density of **266 people per km²** [Worldometer](https://www.worldometers.info/world-population/nigeria-population).

## Why the Number Is Contested

The population figure for Nigeria is **deeply contested** for several interlocking reasons:

### 1. No Census Since 2006
Nigeria last conducted a national census in **2006**, which returned a total of **140 million** [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391). A census was scheduled for **2023** but was **postponed indefinitely** by President Muhammadu Buhari, officially due to the government transition programme and post-election mood [Premium Times](https://www.premiumtimesng.com/news/top-news/595976-real-reasons-census-was-postponed-official.html). As of mid-2026, no new census has been conducted, so all current figures are **extrapolations** from UN/World Bank models, not actual counts [Biometric Update](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202501/nigeria-may-have-got-its-population-wrong-for-two-decades-calls-for-new-biometric-census).

### 2. Historical Pattern of Census Manipulation
Every Nigerian census since independence has been **politically charged and often manipulated** because population figures directly determine **federal revenue allocation, parliamentary seat distribution, and political power between the North and South** [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391):
- **1962 census**: Cancelled because the provisional figure of 45.1 million showed the South had a larger population than the North, upending the North's parliamentary majority. A recount in **1963** magically produced **55.7 million** (a difference of ~11 million), restoring northern dominance [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391).
- **1973 census**: Results (79.8 million, with the North at 64.4%) were discredited and never officialised [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391); [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results).
- **1991 census**: The military government depoliticised the exercise by promising the results would not be used for elections, yielding a more modest **88.5 million** [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391). Even this was marred by allegations of cheating — in some southern states, every single household form listed exactly **nine** people (the maximum the form allowed) [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results).
- **2006 census**: Returned **140 million** (North: ~75 million, South: ~65 million). Bola Tinubu, then-governor of Lagos, rejected the figure of **9.0 million** for Lagos, claiming the state's own parallel count showed **over 17.5 million** [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results). Southern leaders accused the North of inflating numbers to maintain political dominance; northern states rejected southern objections [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results).

### 3. Financial Incentives for Inflation
Nigeria distributes **oil revenue** (over 90% of the country's revenue comes from oil in the South) to states **in proportion to their population**. This creates a powerful incentive for state and local officials to **inflate their population counts** to receive a larger share of federal funds [The Conversation](https://theconversation.com/nigerias-census-has-always-been-tricky-why-this-must-change-150391); [Effective Altruism Forum](https://forum.effectivealtruism.org/posts/824rsHCXuqTmBb8se/nigeria-s-missing-50-million-people). The same incentives drive over-reporting of fertility rates and undercounting of child mortality [Effective Altruism Forum](https://forum.effectivealtruism.org/posts/824rsHCXuqTmBb8se/nigeria-s-missing-50-million-people).

### 4. The "Missing 50 Million" Hypothesis
Some analysts argue that Nigeria's actual population could be **170–180 million**, not the commonly cited 220–240 million — implying a **potential overcount of 50–70 million** [Effective Altruism Forum](https://forum.effectivealtruism.org/posts/824rsHCXuqTmBb8se/nigeria-s-missing-50-million-people). Evidence cited includes:
- **Voter registration and turnout**: In the 2023 presidential election, Nigeria's electoral commission (INEC) had **93 million registered voters**, but only about **25 million votes were cast** — a turnout rate (~27%) that would be implausibly low for a country of 220 million, raising questions about the denominator [Premium Times opinion](https://www.premiumtimesng.com/opinion/589235-nigerias-population-is-a-lot-less-than-220-million-by-tope-fasua.html).
- **National Identification Numbers (NIN)**: By end of 2023, only about **104 million Nigerians** had enrolled for digital IDs, far short of government targets and well below half the claimed population [Biometric Update](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202501/nigeria-may-have-got-its-population-wrong-for-two-decades-calls-for-new-biometric-census).
- **SIM card verification**: A government verification exercise led to the loss of **64.3 million** mobile subscriptions — possibly reflecting nonexistent subscribers on inflated rolls [Effective Altruism Forum](https://forum.effectivealtruism.org/posts/824rsHCXuqTmBb8se/nigeria-s-missing-50-million-people).

However, there is no consensus. Nigeria's Statistician-General Yemi Kale has stated that claims of 170 million are themselves speculation, arguing that extrapolating from a fixed 3% growth rate since 2006 is flawed because social changes (e.g., later marriage, more women in education) make the actual number unknowable without a proper census [The Guardian Nigeria](https://guardian.ng/news/nigerias-population-not-170-million-says-kale).

### 5. Logistical and Security Challenges
Conducting a census in Nigeria is physically difficult — **rough terrain**, **lack of roads**, enumerators needing to use unsafe boats in riverine areas, and thousands walking off the job due to non-payment in 2006 [PRB](https://www.prb.org/resource/objections-surface-over-nigerian-census-results). The ongoing **Boko Haram insurgency** and other security threats in the northeast further complicate enumeration [Biometric Update](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202501/nigeria-may-have-got-its-population-wrong-for-two-decades-calls-for-new-biometric-census).

### 6. Plans for a Biometric Census
In response to these problems, the National Population Commission (NPC) under chairman Nasir Isa Kwarra has announced plans for a **biometric census**, with a proposed **2025 budget of 18.2 billion Naira (~$11.7 million)** for the effort. Kwarra has stated he hopes such a census could resolve accusations of "the riverine people counting fishes and the North counting cows" [Biometric Update](https://www.biometricupdate.com/202501/nigeria-may-have-got-its-population-wrong-for-two-decades-calls-for-new-biometric-census). However, as of mid-2026, the census has not yet been conducted.

## Summary

In short: **no one knows Nigeria's true population**. The headline figures (237–242 million) are UN/World Bank model-based estimates, not census counts. The last actual count was in 2006. A long history of politically motivated census rigging, powerful state-level financial incentives to inflate numbers, voter turnout anomalies, and ID registration shortfalls all point to possible significant overcounting. The next biometric census — repeatedly promised and postponed — may be years away. Until then, any population figure for Nigeria carries a **wide margin of uncertainty** that is unusual for a country of its size and importance.

[Note: stopped early — time limit reached]
